title: Malignant Pleural Mesothelioma
chief_complaint: Chest pain and shortness of breath for 3 months. I worked in shipyards for 30 years with asbestos. I've lost 20 pounds without trying.

physical_exam.Lungs: Dullness to percussion right base. Decreased breath sounds right side.
physical_exam.Chest: Right-sided chest wall pain on palpation.

hidden_data.cxr: Right-sided pleural effusion (large). Pleural thickening with nodularity. Right hemithorax volume loss.
hidden_data.ct_chest: Circumferential nodular pleural thickening encasing right lung (pleural rind). Mediastinal pleural involvement. Chest wall invasion. Right pleural effusion.
hidden_data.pleural_fluid: Exudative (protein 4.5, LDH 450). Cytology: Malignant mesothelial cells.
hidden_data.biopsy: Thoracoscopic pleural biopsy: Epithelioid malignant mesothelioma. Calretinin positive, WT-1 positive.
hidden_data.immunohistochemistry: Calretinin +, WT-1 +, CK5/6 +, TTF-1 -, CEA - (rules out lung adenocarcinoma)

correct_diagnosis: Malignant Pleural Mesothelioma (Epithelioid Type) - Stage III
key_learning_points: Mesothelioma = primary pleural malignancy from asbestos exposure. Latency 20-50 years. No safe level of asbestos exposure
Symptoms: Pleuritic chest pain (90%), dyspnea, weight loss. Pleural effusion + pleural thickening on CT
Diagnosis: Pleural biopsy (thoracoscopic) with IHC. Calretinin, WT-1, CK5/6 positive. TTF-1 and CEA negative (differentiates from lung adenocarcinoma)
Prognosis: Median survival 8-14 months without treatment. Epithelioid type has best prognosis (vs sarcomatoid/biphasic)

Treatment: NOT curable. Pleurectomy/decortication or extrapleural pneumonectomy (controversial). Chemotherapy: Pemetrexed + Cisplatin. Immunotherapy: Nivolumab + Ipilimumab (CheckMate-743). Palliative pleurodesis for recurrent effusion
